content_of_post_to_comment:
It's been a while, but I'm the guy who has been posting about the lifestyle device I'm making to help myself and others accomplish more and live more intentionally.

This is the end of the fourth week of me testing it, and although a lot of bugs and battery issues have made things difficult in between I've used it for myself every single day. The results have been overall very positive in ways I didn't expect. Actually today I just posted on youtube about hitting four consecutive weeks of running two miles every morning (I started off walking but my cardio has gotten much better). I've also lost over 5 lbs during my cut and I've finished listening through two of Alex Hormozi's audiobooks about business. I've still wasted my fair share of time, and some days are better than others, but I can feel the difference in how I want to use my time every day.

None of that is very new though as I have been posting about the benefits I've been seeing for a bit now. The thing that really threw me off was a couple days ago, when I had posted a somewhat successful video early on in the day and decided to take the rest of the night off to watch some tv, which I hadn't done in a while. I found a new show reccomended on tiktok, and It was genuinely really good, but after the first episode I felt weird. I have spent most days trying to make good use of my time, and when I haven't been working I've been spending time with my family, fishing, or working on hobbies. Sitting down and just watching tv by myself felt odd, and I tried watching another episode (again, really enjoyed the series it was very entertaining) and immediately afterwards felt like "okay time to go actually do something".

I don't know why exactly and I had no problem justifying watching a little tv after a bit, but the best way I could describe it is the entertainment felt "cheap". Like I enjoyed the show good enough in the moment, but it didn't reach the deep level of satisfaction I've been getting from other things I guess. I doubt this means I'll stop watching tv or that show eventually, but I want to know if other people have experienced this same type of feeling in your effort to become more disciplined.
comment: I’ve had that same shift after tightening up my days. Stuff like TV or scrolling doesn’t feel bad, it just feels a bit flat compared to things with effort behind them. But I also noticed if I push it too far, I start treating everything as ‘should be productive’ and that gets weird fast. Curious if you ever find a middle ground where leisure still feels fully enjoyable?
